title: Repo-local hook wiring is sticky: once opted in, upgrades refresh hooks without the flag
action: In onboard_bee.mjs main(), before building options, read the repo's existing .bee/onboarding.json; if managed.repo_hooks is a non-empty record, force repoHooks=true for this run (dry-run and apply alike). The flag then means 'opt in', not 'opt in again every time'. Keep the flag working for first-time opt-in. Add tests in scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs: (a) a repo with recorded repo_hooks gets hooks refreshed by a bare --apply, (b) a repo with no repo_hooks record is unaffected by a bare --apply (no hooks vendored), (c) the sticky path picks up a NEW hook file added to source since the last onboard.
must_haves: a repo that previously opted into repo-local hooks gets them refreshed on a bare --apply
a repo that never opted in is unchanged by a bare --apply
new hook files added to source since last onboard are picked up on the sticky path
stale vendored hooks can no longer coexist with a fresh doctrine block reporting up_to_date

trace.verify_output: test_onboard_bee.mjs -> PASS, failures: 0 (6 new sticky cases); test_lib.mjs -> 224 passed, 0 failed. Load-bearing check: with the fix line reverted, exactly 2 sticky cases fail ('a bare --apply refreshes vendored hooks once the repo has opted in' :: guard length after: 29; 'a hook missing from the repo is restored by a bare --apply' :: missing: bee-model-guard.mjs) — the two real-world symptoms — while the never-opted-in case stays green in both directions.
